skills.summary: Use when adding or changing an mcp-vertex plugin so tools, schemas, state, path containment, and durability rules stay correct.
Use when a tool returns ok:false or a swarm stalls to recover from lock conflicts, corruption, state drift, policy blocks, or timeouts.
Use at session start to call overview first, read recommendedNextAction, and choose the right preset before touching plugin-specific tools.
Use as the compact entrypoint for proposal work when you need the canonical swarm workflow without re-deriving the process.
Use for end-to-end proposal execution: orient, select, claim, implement, validate, close, and sync without polling or hand-editing state.
Use when several agents share this repo to choose locks, await release notifications, and avoid duplicate MCP reads.
Use when proposals state is inconsistent after crashes or stale locks to diagnose issues and repair queue, claims, and index drift.
Use before verbose tools or when context grows too fast to keep sessions compact through compact calls, delegation, and metrics-aware discipline.
Use when concurrent agents or processes may touch the same files to choose between withFileMutex and agent_lock or agent_worktree.
Use before creating an audit proposal to follow the mandatory reading order, evidence bar, and output contract for full repo audits.
Use before release-facing commits or bun run release so commit type, derived semver bump, and publish flow stay aligned with automation.
Use as the pointer to the canonical token-budget playbook whenever you need the current guidance for keeping sessions compact.
Use when the status-marker plugin is loaded to choose the right close state, generate the canonical marker, and validate the final line.
Use when running or consolidating audits to follow the tool-level lifecycle, rubric, and ready-versus-done decision flow.
Use before run_quality or apply_rules to understand command resolution, commandPolicy boundaries, and why a rules plan was selected.
Use when adding a rules preset, refactoring internals, or auditing the rules plugin so its SOLID boundaries remain intact.
Use when deciding which lint or typecheck command wins; it explains the priority order project, then dogma, then the vendored default.
Use only when migrating pre-f00016 pNNN proposals so the legacy mapping and required script order stay correct.
Use when adding Astro tabs or brand-marked tabs to reuse the shared Tabs component and brandLogo resolver for public logos.
